name: Lawyer's Free Bankruptcy Advice
description_short: California bankruptcy law firm specializing in affordable Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 representation, with attorney fees starting at $1,000 for simple cases.
description_long:
Bayer, Wishman & Leotta is a bankruptcy-focused law firm operating since 1989, based in California and serving the Los Angeles, Long Beach, San Fernando Valley, Riverside, Orange County, San Bernardino, Ventura, Santa Barbara, and San Francisco Bay areas. The firm has represented approximately 15,000 bankruptcy clients over its 35-year history and reports discharging about one billion dollars in client debt. The firm's core philosophy centers on providing quality bankruptcy representation at reduced fees for straightforward cases, positioning itself as a low-cost alternative to traditional bankruptcy attorneys.

The firm offers free initial consultations conducted personally by named attorneys Jeff Wishman or Leon Bayer (not paralegals), pre-qualification assessments, and representation in Chapter 7 bankruptcy, Chapter 13 bankruptcy, bankruptcy litigation, bankruptcy appeals, and debt negotiation. For simple cases—defined as those with average income, no real estate or valuable assets, no business ownership, no asset transfer issues, current car and rent payments, and six or fewer creditors—the firm quotes starting fees of $1,000 plus the $338 court filing fee and approximately $35 for mandatory credit counseling courses. The firm explicitly states it handles bankruptcy exclusively and does not take divorce, traffic, or other case types.

The firm distinguishes itself through three principal attorneys certified as Bankruptcy Specialists by the State Bar of California, a 35-year operating history, transparent "simple case" criteria that clients can self-assess, and an explicit commitment to avoiding hidden add-on fees (warning clients about "crooked lawyers" who do this). The firm offers flexible service delivery via phone and email to reduce client travel requirements. Leadership attorneys Leon Bayer and Jeffrey Wishman are identified by name throughout marketing materials, and clients are directed to search their names to verify credentials and read reviews.

The primary caveat is that this firm operates as a for-profit law business with financial incentive to classify cases as "simple" to justify lower fees, and complex bankruptcies are handled at higher rates "kept reasonable" but quoted individually. The firm's marketing language heavily emphasizes affordability and quality but lacks third-party verification of success rates, client satisfaction metrics beyond directing searches to Google, or specific outcome data. No information is provided about attorney response times, case timeline estimates, or fee structures for complex cases.
pros: Free initial consultation conducted by a named bankruptcy attorney (not paralegal), with personal involvement by Jeff Wishman or Leon Bayer
Transparent pricing for simple cases starting at $1,000 attorney fee plus filing costs, with explicit warning against hidden add-on charges
Bankruptcy specialization exclusively since 1989—no diluted practice areas or generalist handling
All three principal attorneys are State Bar of California Certified Bankruptcy Specialists
Clear written criteria for "simple cases" allowing prospective clients to self-assess case complexity before consulting
Approximately 15,000 clients served and one billion dollars in debt discharged—demonstrates substantial track record
Flexible service delivery options (phone, email) to reduce in-person office requirements
Handles both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcies, plus bankruptcy litigation, appeals, and debt negotiation alternatives
cons: Pricing is transparently disclosed only for 'simple' cases; complex case fees are quoted individually without published ranges, making cost comparison difficult
Marketing materials rely heavily on self-promotion and directing clients to Google searches rather than providing third-party verification, client testimonials, or published case outcomes
Financial incentive structure may create pressure to classify borderline cases as 'simple' to justify lower fees, though firm disclaims this
No published information on average case timelines, attorney response times, or success/discharge rates for any case type
Limited geographic service area (California only) despite national website domain suggestion
best_for: California consumers with straightforward Chapter 7 bankruptcies, average income, minimal assets, and no business or asset transfer complications seeking affordable legal representation
Individuals unable to afford traditional bankruptcy attorney fees ($3,000-$5,000+) who qualify for the firm's 'simple case' criteria and need personal attorney consultation
Debtors seeking Chapter 13 repayment plan representation or bankruptcy alternatives (debt negotiation) with attorney-led guidance rather than paralegal or app-based services
diagnosis: Best for California residents with straightforward bankruptcy cases who meet the firm's 'simple case' criteria (average income, minimal assets, no business history, few creditors) and need affordable attorney-led representation rather than paralegal or online services. Primary caveat: transparent pricing applies only to simple cases; complex matters require individual quotes, and the firm's marketing relies on self-promotion rather than third-party verification or published outcome metrics, making it difficult to independently assess quality or compare value against other bankruptcy attorneys.
services: Free initial consultation with named bankruptcy attorney
Debt negotiation and settlement in lieu of bankruptcy
Credit counseling course referral and coordination ($35 approximate cost)
Flexible phone and email-based case handling to reduce office visits
Assistance with bankruptcy court representation in Los Angeles, Long Beach, San Fernando Valley, Riverside, Orange County, San Bernardino, Ventura, Santa Barbara, and San Francisco Bay areas
